title: Longest AND Subarray

content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Consider the sequence containing the integers 1,2,…,N1, 2, \dots, N1,2,…,N in increasing order (each exactly once). Find the length of the longest subarray in this sequence such that the bitwise AND of all elements in the subarray is **positive**.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single integer NNN on a single line.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the length of the longest subarray that satisfy the given property.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the length of the longest subarray whose bitwise AND is positive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 the only subarray is [1] (AND=1). For N=2 any subarray longer than 1 contains 1 whose AND becomes 0, so answer 1. For N=3 the subarray [2,3] has AND=2, length 2. For N=4 the best is [2,3] (AND=2) length 2. For N=7 the interval [4,7] has all numbers with the 3rd bit set, AND=4, length 4.

N=5: p=4, len1=2, len2=2, answer 2 (subarray [4,5]). N=8: p=8, len1=1, len2=4, answer 4 (subarray [4,7]). N=10: p=8, len1=3, len2=4, answer 4 (subarray [4,7] is longer than any subarray starting at 8).

solution.approach: The AND of a subarray is positive iff there exists a bit that is set in every number of the subarray. For any bit k, the numbers that have this bit set form a contiguous block [2^k, 2^{k+1}-1]. The longest possible subarray with a positive AND is therefore the longest overlap of the interval [1, N] with one of these power‑of‑two blocks. The best block is the one whose left border is the highest power of two <= N (call it p). Two candidates appear: 1) the block ending at N, length = N - p + 1, and 2) the previous block [p/2, p-1], length = p/2. The answer is max(N-p+1, p/2). Computing p is O(1) using Long.highestOneBit. Hence each test case is solved in constant time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
